2606.10654.title: Speaker Group Encoding in Self-supervised Speech Recognition Models
2606.10654.paper_summary: We investigate what self-supervised speech recognition models (S3Ms) learn about speaker groups (SGs). We examine several states of S3Ms: pretrained, finetuned on speaker identification (SID), finetuned on automatic speech recognition (ASR), and ASR-finetuned using a fairness enhancing algorithm. We find that S3Ms encode information about several speaker group categories (SGCs), including their gender, age, dialect, ethnicity, and whether they are a native speaker. We find that finetuning for SID amplifies certain SGCs, namely those whose variance is more phonetic in nature, though it does not amplify other SGCs, namely those whose variance is more semantic in nature. On the other hand, finetuning for ASR discards phonetically variant speaker group information (SGI) but retains semantically variant SGI. We find that ASR algorithms designed for fairness improvement change to what extent SGI is encoded in S3Ms; however, this is primarily true for for phonetically variant SGCs, and less true for semantically variant SGCs. We discuss how SGI is encoded by each layer, and identify subdimensions of embeddings responsible for encoding different SGCs. Finally, we discuss how our findings could be beneficial in designing fairer ASR algorithms.
2606.10654.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查自我監督語音識別模型（S3Ms）對說話者群體（SGs）學習了什麼。我們檢查了幾種S3Ms的狀態：預訓練、在說話者識別（SID）上微調、在自動語音識別（ASR）上微調，以及使用公平性增強算法進行ASR微調。我們發現S3Ms編碼了有關幾個說話者群體類別（SGCs）的信息，包括他們的性別、年齡、方言、種族，以及他們是否是母語者。我們發現，對SID的微調放大了某些SGCs，特別是那些變異性更具語音特徵的SGCs，儘管它並未放大其他SGCs，特別是那些變異性更具語義特徵的SGCs。另一方面，對ASR的微調則丟棄了語音變異的說話者群體信息（SGI），但保留了語義變異的SGI。我們發現，旨在改善公平性的ASR算法改變了SGI在S3Ms中的編碼程度；然而，這主要適用於語音變異的SGCs，而對語義變異的SGCs則不太適用。我們討論了每一層如何編碼SGI，並確定了負責編碼不同SGCs的嵌入子維度。最後，我們討論了我們的發現如何有助於設計更公平的ASR算法。
2606.10654.author: Felix Herron et.al.
2606.10654.authors: Felix Herron, Solange Rossato Alexandre Allauzen, Benoit Favre, François Portet
